items.front: Manual coordination: when automated coordination is unavailable, what two annotation methods are authorized?
items.back: The Coordination Menu or a flight progress strip, in accordance with facility directives. Per 13-1-6(a).

items.front: Coordination Menu: when is yellow coding removed after a flight plan change?
items.back: After any appropriate action has been taken regarding the flight plan change. Not immediately upon entry, not left indefinitely. Per 13-1-6(b).

items.front: Holding instructions: five approved annotation methods
items.back: Hold View, Hold Data Menu, hold message, flight progress strip, or facility-approved worksheet. Use in accordance with facility directives. Per 13-1-7.

items.front: Free Text Area: three key limitations
items.back: 1. Must remain open and visible while in use. 2. Must be updated or deleted when no longer relevant. 3. Information does NOT pass on handoff and must be coordinated if necessary. For reference purposes only. Per 13-1-8(d), NOTE, (e).

items.front: Free Text Area: five authorized sources for abbreviations
items.back: 1. TBL 13-1-1 clearance abbreviations. 2. TBL 13-1-2 miscellaneous abbreviations. 3. TBL 13-1-3 EDST equivalents. 4. Plain language markings when helpful. 5. Locally approved abbreviations. Per 13-1-8(c).

items.front: Verbal point out: ACL action on approval
items.back: Remove the yellow color coding on the ACL. Per 13-1-8(b).
Must be used in AUTOMATIC mode (IAFDOF Manual must remain deselected) unless otherwise authorized in a facility directive. Per 13-1-9(a).

items.front: IAFDOF/UTM/EDCT: when may coding be acknowledged and removed?
items.back: ONLY after the appropriate action has been completed. Not when the notification first appears, not in advance of action completion. Per 13-1-9(e).

items.front: ERT and RAN: track control requirement
items.back: Do not send/acknowledge ERT or remove RAN coding unless the sector has TRACK CONTROL or has otherwise coordinated. First sector displaying ERT handles it before handoff. Per 13-1-9(f-g).

items.front: Trajectory model: what four data elements are used to model an individual aircraft's trajectory?
items.back: 1. Route of flight. 2. Assigned and interim altitudes. 3. Application/removal of an adapted restriction. 4. Aircraft type. Per 13-1-10 Note 2.

items.front: Interim altitude entry: when may an exception be authorized?
items.back: Only for certain ARTCC sectors, and only if EXPLICITLY DEFINED in an appropriate facility directive. Not at controller discretion. Per 13-1-10(b).
